_doc: Welcome drip sequence — sent per-subscriber based on days since signup. The owned nurture-to-Consent-Resolve funnel. Runner wraps body in a branded template + appends unsubscribe footer.

emails.subject: You're in — here's the contractor toolkit 🛠️
emails.heading: Welcome to Booked Job
emails.body:
Glad you're here. Booked Job is one thing: no-fluff help for the people running the trucks — getting more booked jobs, pricing right, getting paid, and not getting ripped off by lead resellers.

No spam, no pitch. Just what works in the field, a couple times a month.

Start with the free tools — including a calculator that shows your real cost per Angi lead:

emails.subject: The $1,400 lead nobody talks about
emails.heading: Why your Angi leads cost more than you think
emails.body:
Quick math that surprises most shops: Angi, HomeAdvisor and Thumbtack sell the same lead to 5–12 contractors. So once you factor in low close rates, your real cost per booked job runs ~$1,430–$2,500 — versus about $290 for a job won through your own name.

The deeper problem: stop paying and the leads vanish. You rented an audience instead of building one.

emails.subject: A 50% markup is only 33% profit
emails.heading: The pricing mistake that quietly costs you
emails.body:
Most contractors aren't underpriced because they're cheap — they're underpriced because they confuse markup with margin.

A 50% markup feels like 50% profit. It's actually a 33% margin. Across a year of estimates, that gap is serious money. The fix is one formula: price = cost ÷ (1 − target margin).

emails.subject: The cheapest marketing you're skipping
emails.heading: Reviews decide who gets the call
emails.body:
Google reviews decide who shows up in the map pack and who gets the call — yet most shops 'mean to ask' and never do.

The system that works: ask in person the second the job's done (peak gratitude), then text the direct review link within the hour. One tap. Ask every customer.

emails.subject: What if the leads were already on your site?
emails.heading: Stop renting your leads
emails.body:
Here's the thread running through everything we send: the most profitable leads aren't the ones you buy — they're the ones that are already yours.

Most people who visit your website leave without calling. Identifying the ones who consent — and following up before they call the next guy — turns traffic you already paid for into booked jobs. That's the idea behind Consent Resolve: leads that are yours, never resold.

We built it because we got tired of watching good shops rent their customers. If that's a problem you've got, take a look:
